title: dashboard.overview.gpu.detail.full
description: Per-GPU 'details page' (SPA Overview2/GPU/GPUsDetails.vue). Sections envelope with three keys: `detail` (HAMI /v1/gpu basic info, single-item leaf with `device_no` / `driver_version` merged in from the power range query's metric labels), `gauges` (6 instant-vector gauges in fixed order: alloc_core / alloc_mem / util_core / util_mem / power / temperature), `trends` (4 range-vector trends in fixed order: alloc_trend [core+memory] / usage_trend [core+memory] / power_trend / temp_trend). Default time window is 8h ending now; override with --since/--start/--end. `meta.window` carries {since,start,end,step}; `meta.warnings` collects per-query soft failures (any single query 5xx/timeout populates the failed item's `raw.error` and adds a `gauges <key>: ...` or `trends <key>: ...` entry to warnings, the rest of the envelope still renders). HAMI 404 -> empty_reason='no_vgpu_integration'; 5xx on /v1/gpu -> 'vgpu_unavailable' (meta.error / meta.http_status); 200 with empty body -> 'no_gpu_detected'. Soft advisory (non-admin / no CUDA node) lands in meta.note + stderr in table mode.

since.description: Relative window like '8h' / '1h' (empty when --start/--end drove the window)

start.description: ISO-style 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss' in the active timezone

warnings.description: Per-query soft-failure messages (e.g. 'gauges "util_core": HAMI returned HTTP 502'). Empty / missing when all 10 queries succeeded.

detail.description: Single-item envelope wrapping HAMI's /v1/gpu body. `items[0].raw` is the HAMI document verbatim plus `device_no` / `driver_version` (extracted from the power range query's metric labels — the SPA does the same).

gauges.description: Six gauges in fixed SPA order. Each item.raw carries {key, title, unit, query, value, used, total, percent} and (on failure) `error`. Keys are stable: alloc_core / alloc_mem / util_core / util_mem / power / temperature.

trends.description: Four trends in fixed SPA order: alloc_trend (core+memory), usage_trend (core+memory), power_trend (power), temp_trend (temperature). Each item.raw carries {key, title, unit, lines: [{label, query, labels?, points: [{timestamp, value}]}]}; `labels` may include device_no / driver_version harvested from HAMI's series metric.
